value_raw: Legal, cheap, deeply culturally normalized — bia hơi and regional beer identity (Huda in the central region)
notes: A genuine, positive contrast with the cannabis regime's severity

$700-1,100/month realistic all-in per nomad-guide aggregates; $1,000-1,300/month wider comfortable-lifestyle range; broadly similar band to Chiang Mai's own cross-checked ~$900-950/month figure, not independently reconciled line-by-line
Toong Da Nang specifically (most professional option) runs $80-120/month

value_raw: In-city — Vinmec International Hospital (JCI Gold Seal-accredited) + Hoan My Hospital, both central
notes: Both hospitals sit within central Da Nang, a real short distance from An Thuong though not independently measured in km/minutes this pass

value_raw: Concentrated in An Thuong — multiple named coworking spaces (Toong, Base, Enouvo Island)
notes: No hard headcount found; a real gap

value_raw: Son Tra and Ngu Hanh Son districts (adjacent to An Thuong/My Khe beach)
notes: Da Nang is named as one of Vietnam's most open cities for foreign buyers

fact_label: Healthcare system capability — complex/specialist care ceiling
value_raw: Complex oncology, rare neurological disorders, major trauma, and complex surgery generally require medevac — Da Nang triages, doesn't terminally treat these cases
notes: Da Nang lacks the sub-specialist depth found in Hanoi/HCMC

value_raw: Joint Commission International (JCI) Gold Seal-accredited private hospital (Vinmec) + Hoan My deliver Western-standard care across most specialties plus a real 24-hour ER
notes: Internal medicine, surgery, obstetrics, pediatrics, cardiology, cancer care, bariatric surgery, minimally invasive cardiac procedures all named as available in-city

FPT fiber — among the strongest advertised residential broadband value found anywhere in this project's roster; coworking/cafe speeds 50-100 Mbps standard. No measured (vs. advertised) speed-test data found

value_raw: Yes

Furnished studios specifically $300-450/mo — sits at or below Numbeo's outside-centre range, unlike Goa's Assagao or Phuket's Bang Tao, which run premium relative to their respective citywide figures

value_raw: Doesn't map onto a freehold-compound model — group could plausibly buy multiple units in one building, subject to the 30% quota
notes: A genuinely different shape of "settling together" than Guatemala's/Belize's adjacent-parcel freehold model — not independently tested against a real group scenario

fact_label: Subjective community read — concentrated, purpose-built nomad district
value_raw: More deliberately clustered and walkable than Goa's Anjuna-Assagao corridor or Phuket's family/lifestyle-enclave split
notes: Da Nang's nomad geography reads smaller and more concentrated than either comparison candidate

value_raw: Extremely rare — one of the safest cities in Vietnam/SE Asia, well-patrolled tourist areas
notes: Regarded by both expats and Vietnamese nationals as the safest, most livable city in the country

value_raw: General Secretary + President combined in one person for the first time in 4 decades; renewed 5-year mandate, retirement-age exemption
notes: New Regulation 196 (June 2026) further strengthens party-committee authority inside state-owned firms

fact_label: Any Temporary Residence Card (TRC) route converts to Permanent Residence / naturalization
value_raw: [GAP]
notes: Vietnam has a PR-card mechanism in principle, but no clear, sourced eligibility timeline or in-practice process description was found this pass

value_raw: Normal, current, tolerated — no active crackdown, cumulative-day cap, or pattern-based enforcement found
notes: A real, meaningful contrast with Thailand's 2025-2026 tightening — but explicitly named as current-as-of-this-pass, not permanent (border-run tolerance can shift with little notice, per Thailand's own precedent)

value_raw: Illegal, death penalty for trafficking 5kg+ resin / 75kg+ leaves-stems-seeds (Art. 251); 2-7yr imprisonment for simple possession 1-500g (Art. 249)
notes: Materially harsher than Goa's cannabis regime — applies to foreigners exactly as to Vietnamese nationals, no exception

value_raw: [GAP]
source_ref: [GAP — genuine, unresolved sourcing conflict, not resolved this pass]
notes: One source states a one-time 90-day extension is officially available; most other sources say extension beyond 90 days is generally not available and exit-reapply is standard practice instead
Applies to all countries/territories as of 2026; single- or multi-entry; USD 25 fee. Longer than Thailand's post-rollback 30-day exemption, shorter than Malaysia's fee-free 90-day exemption

value_raw: A real, current draw — 2024 US FDI stock $4.4B, up 8% from 2023, driven by China-cost/tariff-driven supply-chain diversification
notes: Named frictions remain real: high tariffs on food/agricultural products, limited regulatory transparency, inadequate IP protection
Once reached, no further units in that building can be sold to foreigners — the same structural shape as Thailand's 49% condo cap, just a lower number

fact_label: Foreign-ownership quota — houses per ward-equivalent unit
notes: A second, separate cap layered on top of the 30% building-unit quota

fact_label: Foreigner access to public healthcare system
value_raw: No — expats ineligible for subsidized public insurance; public facilities usable only at full non-subsidized cash rate
notes: Work-permit holders are compulsorily enrolled in Vietnam's social health insurance (VSS) as a labor-law condition of the permit, but coverage is public-hospital-only with low reimbursement — described as "essentially irrelevant" for expats using private care in practice. No visa or Temporary Residence Card (TRC) route found this pass conditions the visa itself on insurance proof (contrast Thailand's O-A mandate)

value_raw: Not yet checked
notes: Same `HardConstraintRule`-shaped mechanism category as Thailand's condo-quota system; no lived account of the quota system, renewal process, or resale-inheritance mechanic found this pass

fact_label: No retirement, passive-income, or digital-nomad visa exists
value_raw: Yes (confirmed absent)
notes: Long-term legal stay rests entirely on a Temporary Residence Card (TRC) tied to a qualifying purpose (employment, investment, marriage/family, sponsored org work) — confirmed directly, not just inherited from an earlier scan

value_raw: Durably controlled; genuine political stability expected precisely because dissent is suppressed and power is centralized
notes: Not a fragile-coalition kind of stability the way several other candidates show — worth naming both the stability and the authoritarian governance model plainly

notes: General understanding is fines/deportation/blacklisting apply, but no specific fine schedule or ban-duration ladder confirmed this pass

fact_label: Pet import (dog and cat) — rabies titer / quarantine requirement
value_raw: Rabies vaccination 30 days–12 months before travel; 15-digit ISO microchip required; quarantine status genuinely conflicting across sources
notes: A fourth exception to this project's dog-only pet-import gap (both species researched together). One source says quarantine "usually not required" with valid paperwork; another says pets "usually undergo quarantine," 7-21 days — a real, unresolved conflict, treat as a live risk. Import permit required only for 3+ pets

fact_label: Private/international health insurance — annual premium range
notes: Local Vietnam-only plans ~$200-1,000/yr; regional ASEAN plans ~$600-1,200/yr; full international plans $1,500+/yr; one cross-provider average cited at ~$4,547/yr (~$380/month) for an individual plan

fact_label: Private/international health insurance — new-enrollment age ceiling
notes: William Russell: under 76 at policy start; HSBC/Bao Viet: new enrollment to 69, extendable to 75 with continuous renewal; Feather: coverage advertised to age 75. Gates *starting* coverage only — plans generally renew indefinitely once enrolled. Region-typical, not unusually harsh

value_raw: closed-except-single-vehicle
notes: Flagged: no primary statute number found in this project's Vietnam research this pass — the composite-name fallback per §4's own grammar. A property.md doesn't exist for Vietnam yet either.

value_raw: Resale buyer inherits the remaining lease term, not a fresh 50 years — clock starts at original certificate issuance
notes: A real, easy-to-miss detail for anyone considering a resale purchase rather than new-build

value_raw: No
notes: Genuinely different from both India's near-total bar and Guatemala's/Mexico's freehold models — a defined, if term-limited, purchasable interest exists here. No mechanism_legality companion row added — this is a structural absence of a freehold option, not a punished workaround.

value_raw: Yes (50yr, renewable once to 100yr total)
notes: Not a freehold purchase of land — buying a condo means buying the right to occupy the unit for the lease period. Two separate quotas layered on top: 30% of units per building, 250 houses per ward-equivalent unit. Resale buyer inherits the remaining lease term, not a fresh 50 years.

value_raw: legitimate

fact_label: Temporary Residence Card (TRC) via Employment — structure
value_raw: Requires a Vietnamese work permit; a Temporary Residence Card (TRC) is then issued for 6 months to 5 years depending on underlying category
notes: Not applicable without a genuine qualifying Vietnamese (or foreign-invested Vietnam-based) employer

fact_label: Temporary Residence Card (TRC) via Employment accepts passive income as qualifying proof
value_raw: No
notes: Active employment only — no passive-income equivalent

fact_label: Temporary Residence Card (TRC) via Marriage/Family — precondition-gated
value_raw: Real but conditional — requires a Vietnamese-citizen spouse/family member
notes: Not independently researched to the mechanical depth of Thailand's marriage-visa section (financial thresholds, renewal cadence, PR-equivalent eligibility) — a named gap

fact_label: Temporary Residence Card (TRC) via Registered Investment — capital threshold
value_raw: [GAP]
source_ref: [GAP — specific capital thresholds not confirmed with a primary source this pass]
notes: A qualifying registered investment (e.g. establishing/investing in a Vietnamese company) qualifies, but no consistent minimum-capital figure found, unlike Thailand's clearly tiered ฿3M/฿10M/$1M structure
